title: React from Scratch
description: Build a working React app without the CLI noise. We start with a single HTML file, add JSX, component composition, hooks, and finish with a small router — all in your browser console.

lessons.title: Hello, JSX
lessons.content: JSX is just function calls. Every tag becomes a React.createElement call.

lessons.title: Components and props
lessons.content: A component is a function that returns markup. Props are the arguments.

lessons.title: useState and useEffect
lessons.content: State is per-component. Effects run after commit.

lessons.title: Lifting state up
lessons.content: When two siblings need the same data, the parent owns it.

lessons.title: A tiny router, by hand
lessons.content: window.location.pathname + popstate is enough to start.

tasks.title: Build a counter with useState
tasks.question: Create a counter component that increments and decrements.
tasks.instruction: Use the useState hook. No external libraries.
